problem_statement: PR #82272: kernel/sched: Correct k_sleep() return value when a 32-bit tick wraparound occurs. Fixes #79863 The `expected_wakeup_ticks` and `sys_clock_tick_get_32()` are uint32_t values, and may wrap around individually. If the `expected_wakeup_ticks` has a wraparound and `sys_clock_tick_get_32()` doesn't, so `expected_wakeup_ticks < sys_clock_tick_get_32()`, the API return value will be corr
